title: Judge verdict (grades one ScholarEval evaluation)
description: What roles/Judge.md records each iteration: a 0-100 grade derived from the ScholarEval evaluation per the fixed rubric.md, plus a prioritized fix-list handed to the Reviser. One file per iteration at <sandbox_root>/iter<N>/verdict.json.

scores.description: Anchored 0-5 sub-scores per rubric axis (see rubric.md). These are the components of the grade.

soundness.description: Methods well-supported and free of unaddressed contradictions.

contribution.description: Dimensions with genuine, defended novelty and few prior-art weaknesses.

evidence_quality.description: How well the ScholarEval points were actually grounded in retrieved citations (the meta-check).

grade.description: Weighted aggregate per rubric.md, on 0-100. Compared against <pass_threshold>.

gate_failures.description: Hard-gate violations that force pass=false regardless of grade (e.g. an unaddressed contradiction the rubric marks fatal, or grade computed from ungrounded evidence).

fixes.description: Prioritized, actionable items for the Reviser, highest-impact first. Drawn from ScholarEval Suggestions/Contradictions/Weaknesses.

issue.description: The weakness/contradiction, citing the evidence key(s) behind it.

action.description: The concrete change to the proposal that would resolve it.

expected_gain.description: Which sub-score this should move, and roughly how much.

rationale.description: Brief chain-of-thought summary justifying the grade. Filled AFTER the scores.
